A preview of how a proposed CloudFormation template change would affect the resources in an existing stack ??? which resources are created, updated, replaced, or deleted. Execute the change set to apply the change, or discard it.

A software development practice where developers frequently merge code into a shared branch and an automated pipeline builds and tests the change. On AWS: CodeBuild + CodeCommit / CodePipeline, or GitHub Actions / GitLab connecting to AWS via OIDC.

A practice where every change that passes CI is automatically prepared for production release, with a manual approval gate before the deploy. CodePipeline + CodeDeploy is the canonical AWS-native implementation.

A practice that extends continuous delivery by automatically deploying every change that passes CI into production with no manual approval ??? relying on automated tests, canary / blue-green deploys, and rollback to keep risk low.

A container inside an AWS Organization that groups accounts hierarchically. SCPs attached to an OU apply to every member account. OUs can nest up to 5 levels and let you mirror your business structure (Dev / Prod / Sandbox, Finance / Marketing / Engineering).

An AWS Organizations feature that combines the bills of all member accounts into a single management-account invoice. Eligible usage is aggregated to qualify for volume discounts (S3, EC2) and Reserved Instances / Savings Plans can be shared across accounts that have opted in.

An AWS Region disabled by default that you must explicitly enable in your account before using. Currently includes regions in the Middle East, Africa, Asia-Pacific (Hong Kong / Hyderabad / Jakarta / Melbourne), Europe (Milan / Spain / Zurich), and Israel.

Isolated AWS Regions (us-gov-east-1, us-gov-west-1) designed to host sensitive workloads in the cloud for U.S. government agencies and contractors. Operated by U.S. citizens on U.S. soil, supports FedRAMP High, ITAR, DoD SRG IL5; requires a separate AWS GovCloud account.

A central place to view and request quota increases for AWS services across your account. Replaces the older support-ticket workflow for raising service limits and integrates with CloudWatch to alert when usage approaches a quota.

Lets you create logical groupings of AWS resources based on tags or CloudFormation stacks. Used as the target of bulk operations in Systems Manager, CloudWatch dashboards, and tag-based access policies.

AWS's global community of consulting and technology partners that build and resell solutions on AWS. Partners are tiered (Select / Advanced / Premier) and may hold competencies (security, ML, migration) or service-delivery validations. AWS Marketplace is one APN distribution channel.

A managed chaos-engineering service that runs controlled fault-injection experiments against AWS workloads (EC2 stop, EBS pause I/O, RDS reboot, network latency, API throttling). Experiment templates define stop conditions; results integrate with CloudWatch.

A collaborative analytics service that lets companies and partners run joint SQL queries on their combined data without sharing the underlying rows. Encryption, privacy-preserving query controls (aggregation thresholds, query allow-listing), and audit logs keep each party's data confidential.

A managed service for building, monitoring, and managing a unified global network across VPCs, branch offices, data centres, and partner Regions. Replaces the manual mesh of Transit Gateways, Site-to-Site VPNs, and Direct Connect Gateways with policy-driven segments.

The original name for what is now Amazon EventBridge ??? an event bus that routes events from AWS services, custom applications, and SaaS partners to targets (Lambda, Step Functions, SNS, SQS, etc.). The CloudWatch Events name is still recognised in older docs and IAM actions (events:*).

A long-term credential pair (AKIA???/SecretKey) used to sign programmatic AWS API requests. Issued to IAM users (or via root, strongly discouraged). Prefer IAM roles or IAM Identity Center temporary credentials; if you must use access keys, rotate them regularly via IAM Access Analyzer recommendations.

Short-lived AWS credentials issued by STS (15 minutes to 36 hours). Returned by AssumeRole, GetSessionToken, AssumeRoleWithSAML, and AssumeRoleWithWebIdentity. Contain an access key ID, secret key, AND a session token that must accompany every signed request.

A metadata field that controls how long a record or message remains valid. In DynamoDB, an item with a TTL attribute in the past is deleted within 48 hours at no cost. In Route 53, TTL determines DNS resolver caching. In CloudFront, TTL controls how long the edge keeps an object before re-validating with the origin.

Resource tags activated in Billing settings so that their keys appear as columns in the Cost and Usage Report and dimensions in Cost Explorer. Two types: AWS-generated (createdBy) and user-defined. Foundation for showback/chargeback by team, environment, or cost centre.

A documented plan for how an organisation labels AWS resources with tags. Typical dimensions: environment (dev/stage/prod), owner / team, cost centre, data classification, application. Combined with AWS Organizations tag policies and IAM tag-based access control.

AWS publishes its scope-1 and scope-2 emissions via the Customer Carbon Footprint Tool in Billing. Customers are responsible for sustainability of what they build in the cloud ??? right-sizing instances, using managed services, choosing renewable Regions, and applying the Sustainability pillar guidance.

The process and tooling to recover IT systems after a disruption. AWS defines four DR strategies on a cost / RTO spectrum: backup and restore (cheapest, slowest), pilot light, warm standby, and multi-site active-active (most expensive, lowest RTO).

A cloud-architecture model where you write functions or use managed services without provisioning servers; the provider handles scaling, patching, and capacity. AWS serverless primitives include Lambda, Fargate, API Gateway, EventBridge, Step Functions, DynamoDB on-demand, Aurora Serverless, SNS, SQS.

The URL or DNS name of an AWS service entry point. Each Region has its own service endpoint (e.g. dynamodb.eu-west-1.amazonaws.com). VPC endpoints provide private connectivity to services without traversing the public internet.

Splitting large API responses into smaller pages, each returning a token (NextToken, ExclusiveStartKey) to fetch the next page. Standard across AWS APIs and SDKs; the SDK paginators iterate transparently. DynamoDB Query/Scan, S3 ListObjectsV2, Lambda ListFunctions all paginate.

A retry strategy where the wait between attempts grows exponentially (1s, 2s, 4s, 8s ???), usually combined with random jitter to avoid thundering-herd. The AWS SDKs implement it automatically for throttling and 5xx responses; standard recipe for handling AWS API rate-limit errors.

A unique client-supplied identifier (UUID) attached to a request so that retries return the original result instead of creating duplicate side-effects. Required by EC2 RunInstances (ClientToken), API Gateway / Lambda integrations, Step Functions, and many SDK helpers.

An API Gateway endpoint type that routes client requests to the nearest CloudFront edge location and from there to the regional API endpoint. Reduces TLS-handshake and TCP-round-trip latency for geographically dispersed clients.

An API Gateway endpoint type deployed in a single Region with no CloudFront layer. Recommended when clients are in the same Region (lower latency, lower cost) or when you front the API with your own CloudFront distribution.
An API Gateway endpoint accessible only from a VPC via an interface VPC endpoint. Lets you expose APIs internally to applications running in private subnets without crossing the public internet.
